subject: Geology
title: Faults
description: A micro-lesson that makes Faults usable.

hook.text: A fault is a crack in the Earth where blocks of rock have moved past each other. Every earthquake needs one.

buildup.text: Faults are fractures in rock along which movement has occurred. They range from tiny to thousands of kilometers long.

discovery.text: Normal faults pull apart. Reverse faults push together. Strike-slip faults slide sideways past each other.

twist.text: Many faults are hidden underground. Some of the most dangerous ones have no surface expression at all.

climax.text: Faults are the machinery of earthquakes. Every quake that shakes the surface started with movement along a fault.

punchline.text: Every earthquake has a fault line behind it.

quiz.question: What type of fault involves horizontal sliding?
